spec_match.rationale: Uses real Web Serial API calls (navigator.serial.requestPort, port.open with options) and reads getInfo() correctly. It supports both hex framing and raw ASCII transmission.

interactive.rationale: Provides a live, dynamically updated hex binary frame builder (which re-renders hex byte tokens byte-by-byte with checksums) and a working serial terminal.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Matches the blurb, showing an interactive terminal, a byte-by-byte binary frame builder, and getInfo() inspector.

edge_cases.rationale: Gracefully handles invalid hex characters in the builder and logs errors when requesting/writing to non-open ports.

references.rationale: The ChromeStatus link on the protocol-explorer page points to feature ID 5078540898902016 instead of the parent feature ID 5139978918821888 (Web Serial over Bluetooth on Android).

design_system.rationale: Styled with CSS variables, uses good layout spacing, responsive, and has appropriate AA contrast.

openQuestions.title: Correct ChromeStatus reference link
openQuestions.detail: The ChromeStatus link on the protocol-explorer page points to feature ID 5078540898902016 instead of the parent feature ID 5139978918821888 (Web Serial over Bluetooth on Android). Correct this reference.

summary: A beautifully written tool that constructs, visualizes, and sends byte-level binary serial frames live, hampered slightly by linking to an incorrect ChromeStatus ID.
